items.title: Teleport Launches Agentic Identity Framework to Secure AI Agents Across Enterprise Infrastructure
items.contentSnippet:
Teleport recently unveiled the Teleport Agentic Identity Framework, a new AI-centered security model designed to help enterprises safely deploy autonomous and semi-autonomous AI agents across cloud and on-premises environments.
 By Craig Risi
items.creator: Craig Risi

items.title: Podcast: Beyond Code: How Engineers Need to Evolve in the AI Era
items.contentSnippet: In this podcast, Shane Hastie, Lead Editor for Culture & Methods, spoke to Ben Greene about embracing AI in software engineering, expanding beyond pure technical skills to understand business context, and prioritizing human empathy in increasingly au
items.creator: Ben Greene

items.title: Shadcn Releases Visual Project Builder
items.contentSnippet: Introducing Shadcn's innovative visual project builder, accessible via `npx shadcn create`. This tool empowers developers to customize project setups visually before coding, ensuring a seamless design-first approach with real-time previews, extensive
items.creator: Daniel Curtis

items.title: VillageSQL Launches as an Extension-Focused MySQL Fork
items.contentSnippet:
A new open-source project, VillageSQL, has been introduced as a tracking fork of MySQL aimed at expanding extensibility and addressing feature gaps increasingly relevant to AI and agent-based workloads.
 By Robert Krzaczyński
items.creator: Robert Krzaczyński

items.title: OpenAI Scales Single Primary Postgresql to Millions of Queries per Second for ChatGPT
items.contentSnippet: OpenAI described how it scaled PostgreSQL to support ChatGPT and its API platform, handling millions of queries per second for hundreds of millions of users. By running a single-primary PostgreSQL deployment on Azure with nearly 50 read replicas, opt
items.creator: Leela Kumili

items.title: Creating Impactful Teams through Diversity Using Session 0
items.contentSnippet: Diverse and empowered teams are impactful teams, Natan Žabkar Nordberg mentioned in his talk on creating impactful software teams at QCon London. A session 0 helps set expectations and ensures that everyone is approaching the team in a compatible way
items.creator: Ben Linders

items.title: WhatsApp Deploys Rust-Based Media Parser to Block Malware on 3 Billion Devices
items.contentSnippet: WhatsApp has rewritten its media handling library in Rust, replacing 160,000 lines of C++ with 90,000 lines of memory-safe code for 3 billion devices. The rollout, part of a system called Kaleidoscope, uses differential fuzzing to ensure bug-for-bug
items.creator: Steef-Jan Wiggers

items.title: Article: You’ve Generated Your MVP Using AI. What Does That Mean for Your Software Architecture?
items.contentSnippet: AI‑generated code creates implicit architectural decisions, forcing teams to rely on experimentation to validate quality attributes. To get useful results from AI, teams must clearly express trade‑offs and reasoning so the model can generate solution
items.creator: Pierre Pureur, Kurt Bittner

items.title: Presentation: Scaling to 100+ as a Director: Lessons From Growing Engineering Organizations
items.contentSnippet: Thiago Ghisi explains the structural shifts required as an engineering org scales from 30 to 100+ engineers. He shares how to transition from managing performance to building opinionated leadership teams and shaping long-term culture. He discusses hi
items.creator: Thiago Ghisi

items.title: Podcast: [Video Podcast] The Craft of Software Architecture in the Age of AI Tools
items.contentSnippet: AI coding assistants promise speed, but what do they mean for quality, trust, and the architect’s craft? In this inaugural episode of Next Gen Architecture Playbook, Shweta Vohra and Grady Booch explore a principled view of how architecture must evol
items.creator: Grady Booch

items.title: CloudFront Adds Origin mTLS Authentication for End-to-End Zero Trust
items.contentSnippet: Amazon CloudFront now supports mutual TLS authentication for origin servers, completing end-to-end zero-trust authentication from viewers to backends. The feature replaces IP allowlists and shared secrets with cryptographic verification, proving part
items.creator: Steef-Jan Wiggers

items.title: Article: From Prompts to Production: A Playbook for Agentic Development
items.contentSnippet: In this article, author Abhishek Goswami shares a practitioner's playbook with development practices, that describes building agentic AI applications and scaling them in production. He also presents core architecture patterns for agentic application
items.creator: Abhishek Goswami

items.title: Pandas 3.0 Introduces Default String Dtype and Copy-on-Write Semantics
items.contentSnippet: The pandas team has released pandas 3.0.0, a major update that changes core behaviors around string handling, memory semantics, and datetime resolution, while removing a substantial amount of deprecated functionality. The release introduces several c
items.creator: Robert Krzaczyński

items.title: Kubernetes Drives AI Expansion as Cultural Shift Becomes Critical
items.contentSnippet: A new CNCF report identifies Kubernetes as the primary engine for AI growth, with 82% production adoption. However, technical maturity has outpaced organisational change. Human factors, such as siloed team structures and a lack of cross-functional co
items.creator: Mark Silvester

items.title: GitHub Copilot SDK Lets Developers Integrate Copilot CLI's Engine into Apps
items.contentSnippet:
Now available in technical preview on GitHub, the GitHub Copilot SDK lets developers embed the same engine that powers GitHub Copilot CLI into their own apps, making it easier to build agentic workflows.
 By Sergio De Simone
items.creator: Sergio De Simone

items.title: QCon Previews 20th Anniversary Conferences: Production AI, Resilience, and Staff+ Engineering
items.contentSnippet: Celebrating its 20th anniversary, QCon’s 2026 conferences in London and San Francisco will focus on the engineering realities of agentic AI, resilient architectures, and platform ROI. The programs continue the series' two-decade tradition of practiti
items.creator: Artenisa Chatziou

items.title: BellSoft Survey Finds Container Security Practices Are Undermining Developers’ Own Goals
items.contentSnippet:
Container security incidents are becoming a routine reality for software teams, and the tools meant to protect them may be making the problem worse.
 By Craig Risi
items.creator: Craig Risi

items.title: Windsurf Introduces Arena Mode to Compare AI Models During Development
items.contentSnippet: Windsurf has introduced Arena Mode inside its IDE allowing developers to compare large language models side by side while working on real coding tasks. The feature is designed to let users evaluate models directly within their existing development co
items.creator: Daniel Dominguez

items.title: Article: Jakarta EE 12 Milestone 2: Advent of the Data Age Along with Consistency and Configuration
items.contentSnippet: Jakarta EE 12 Milestone 2 marks the beginning of the next generation of enterprise Java. It introduces Jakarta Query, a unified query language across Persistence, Data, and NoSQL, while aligning the platform with Java 21. This milestone focuses on in
items.creator: Otavio Santana

items.title: Waku: the Minimal React Framework Reaches Alpha
items.contentSnippet: Waku 1.0 alpha, a lightweight React framework, achieves a major milestone with stable public APIs, shifting focus to bug fixes and compatibility. Ideal for mostly-static sites, it offers flexible rendering options. Built on Vite and Hono, Waku priori
items.creator: Daniel Curtis

items.title: Rspack Releases Version 1.7: Final 1.x Update Before 2.0 Transition
items.contentSnippet: Rspack 1.7 has launched, enhancing performance and plugin compatibility as it prepares for a major version transition. Key features include improved SWC plugin compatibility, native asset importing as bytes, and default lazy compilation for dynamic m
items.creator: Daniel Curtis

items.title: Presentation: WASM Components are a FaaS' Best Friend
items.contentSnippet: Laurent Doguin shares why Wasm’s cold-start performance and security model make it the ideal FaaS runtime. He discusses the WebAssembly Component Model for polyglot interoperability and explains how to build distributed, provider-based architectures
items.creator: Laurent Doguin

items.title: Article: Autonomous Big Data Optimization: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning to Achieve Self-Tuning Apache Spark
items.contentSnippet: This article introduces a reinforcement learning (RL) approach grounded in Apache Spark that enables distributed computing systems to learn optimal configurations autonomously, much like an apprentice engineer who learns by doing. The author also imp
items.creator: Hina Gandhi

items.title: Podcast: The Technical Founder's Path: Code, Leadership, and Balance
items.contentSnippet:
In this podcast, Shane Hastie, Lead Editor for Culture & Methods, spoke to Trisha Ballakur about transitioning from coder to startup CEO, balancing technical leadership with business development, and avoiding burnout on the journey.
 By Trisha Ballak
items.creator: Trisha Ballakur

items.title: Microsoft Adds Custom Copilot Agents for .NET Developers with C# and WinForms Experts
items.contentSnippet: Microsoft and GitHub have expanded the Copilot ecosystem with the first .NET-focused GitHub Copilot custom agents, designed to improve productivity and code quality for C# and Windows Forms developers. The announcement, part of the broader Copilot cu
items.creator: Edin Kapić

items.title: Anthropic Releases Updated Constitution for Claude
items.contentSnippet: Anthropic has published an updated constitution for Claude, its AI assistant, providing a structured framework that guides behavior, reasoning, and training. The constitution combines explicit principles with contextual guidance, making it a practica
items.creator: Robert Krzaczyński

items.title: Article: Engineering Speed at Scale — Architectural Lessons from Sub-100-ms APIs
items.contentSnippet: Sub‑100-ms APIs emerge from disciplined architecture using latency budgets, minimized hops, async fan‑out, layered caching, circuit breakers, and strong observability. But long‑term speed depends on culture, with teams owning p99, monitoring drift, m
items.creator: Saranya Vedagiri

items.title: Uber Moves from Static Limits to Priority-Aware Load Control for Distributed Storage
items.contentSnippet: Uber engineers detailed how they evolved their storage platform from static rate limiting to a priority-aware load management system. The approach protects Docstore and Schemaless, Uber’s MySQL-based distributed databases, by colocating control with
items.creator: Leela Kumili

items.title: Building Software Organisations Where People Can Thrive
items.contentSnippet: Continuous learning, adaptability, and strong support networks are the foundations for thriving teams, Matthew Card mentioned. Trust is built through consistent, fair leadership and addressing toxic behaviour, bias, and microaggressions early. By fos
items.creator: Ben Linders

items.title: Google DeepMind Introduces ATLAS Scaling Laws for Multilingual Language Models
items.contentSnippet:
Google DeepMind researchers have introduced ATLAS, a set of scaling laws for multilingual language models that formalize how model size, training data volume, and language mixtures interact as the number of supported languages increases.
 By Robert K
items.creator: Robert Krzaczyński

items.title: Presentation: Foundation Models for Ranking: Challenges, Successes, and Lessons Learned
items.contentSnippet: Moumita Bhattacharya discusses the evolution of Netflix’s ranking systems, from the multi-model architecture to a Unified Contextual Recommender (UniCoRn). She explains how they built a task-agnostic User Foundation Model to capture long-term member
items.creator: Moumita Bhattacharya

items.title: Swift Cross-Platform Framework Skip Now Fully Open Source
items.contentSnippet: After three years of development, the team behind Skip, a solution designed to create iOS and Android apps from a single Swift/SwiftUI codebase, has announced their decision to make the product completely and open source, in order to foster adoption
items.creator: Sergio De Simone

items.title: Railway Highlights the Importance of Logs, Metrics, Traces, and Alerts for Diagnosing System Failure
items.contentSnippet:
Railway’s engineering team published a comprehensive guide to observability, explaining how developers and SRE teams can use logs, metrics, traces, and alerts together to understand and diagnose production system failures.
 By Craig Risi
items.creator: Craig Risi

items.title: Google BigQuery Adds SQL-Native Managed Inference for Hugging Face Models
items.contentSnippet: Google has launched SQL-native managed inference for 180,000+ Hugging Face models in BigQuery. The preview release collapses the ML lifecycle into a unified SQL interface, eliminating the need for separate Kubernetes or Vertex AI management. Key feat
items.creator: Steef-Jan Wiggers

items.title: Google Introduces TranslateGemma Open Models for Multilingual Translation
items.contentSnippet: Google has released TranslateGemma, a set of open translation models based on the Gemma 3 architecture, offering 4B, 12B, and 27B parameter variants designed to support machine translation across 55 languages and to run on platforms ranging from mobi
items.creator: Daniel Dominguez
